name: OptionStrat
description_short: Visual options strategy builder, P&L calculator, and real-time unusual options flow scanner with 50+ pre-built strategy templates, combined Greeks, Congress/insider trade tracking, and best-in-class iOS and Android mobile apps.
description_long:
## What OptionStrat Actually Is

OptionStrat is a browser-based and mobile options analysis platform built around three core products: the Strategy Builder, the Options Optimizer, and an Unusual Options Flow scanner. It exists to do one thing — help traders visualise and understand options strategies — and it does that better than any other tool we've used day-to-day.

This review was upgraded in April 2026 after direct verification against optionstrat.com, the /membership pricing page, the about page, the iOS App Store listing, and the Google Play Store listing. Every specific feature, price, rating, and version number below comes from a primary source on the date of upgrade.

## The Three Core Products

**Strategy Builder.** The headline tool and the reason most traders sign up. You construct any combination of calls, puts, and stock positions across multiple expirations and the platform instantly renders a clean visual P&L diagram with breakeven points, max profit, max loss, probability of profit, and combined Greeks for the whole position. The navigation surfaces 50+ pre-built strategy templates across five difficulty tiers — Novice (long calls, cash-secured puts, covered calls), Basic (credit and debit spreads), Intermediate (iron condors, calendar spreads, diagonals), Advanced (ratio spreads, condors, straddles), and Expert (synthetic futures, long combos, jade lizards). The strategy library is comprehensive enough that if you can name it, OptionStrat can build it.

**Options Optimizer.** Give it a ticker, a target price, and a target date, and the Optimizer scans thousands of possible option combinations and ranks them by best expected return or best probability of profit. For traders with a directional thesis who aren't sure whether a long call, a bull call spread, or a bull put spread is the best structure, this is a genuine time-saver — it factors in current IV, skew, and pricing rather than forcing you to model each alternative by hand.

**Unusual Options Flow.** The Live Flow tier adds real-time monitoring of large and unusual options trades, sweep detection, multi-leg complex orders, alert performance tracking, historical search, and — unusually — a dedicated Congress and corporate insider trade tracker. The flow scanner is integrated tightly enough with the strategy builder that you can see an unusual institutional trade, tap it, and immediately model the same structure to understand its risk profile.

## Pricing — Verified on /membership (April 9, 2026)

OptionStrat publishes three tiers at optionstrat.com/membership:

- **Free** — Delayed data, limited access. Full access to the strategy builder with 15-minute delayed pricing, all 50+ pre-built strategies, and basic P&L visualisation. This tier is genuinely useful for learning options mechanics, not just a teaser.
- **Live Tools — $39.99/month or $419.99/year** (annual saves ~12%). Adds real-time stock and options pricing, market events and news, volume overlay on charts, chance of profit calculations, combined Greeks for multi-leg positions, IV adjustments per expiration, saved strategy tracking with history, commission-fee calculations, access to the private Discord community, and 15-minute delayed futures data. 7-day free trial.
- **Live Flow — $99.99/month or $999.99/year.** Adds everything in Live Tools plus the real-time unusual options flow scanner, custom filter creation and saving, mobile and web flow alerts, alert performance tracking, historical flow search, live newsfeed, and Congress + insider trade tracking. 7-day free trial.

A note on the /membership page that's easy to miss: **live data is restricted to individual, non-professional users.** Professional advisors and registered investment persons can subscribe to paid tiers and access everything else, but live market data is off-limits due to exchange licensing — that's an exchange rule, not an OptionStrat decision.

## Mobile Apps — Real Traction on Both Platforms

OptionStrat's mobile experience is genuinely best-in-class for options analysis, and both apps have meaningful real-world traction.

**iOS (App Store).** Listed as "OptionStrat: Options Toolkit" from OptionStrat LLC. As of April 9, 2026 the app carries a **4.82/5 rating across 1,927 ratings**, current version 1.2.57, originally released December 3, 2020, last updated April 8, 2025. That's one of the higher-quality ratings we've seen for any trading app.

**Android (Google Play).** Listed as "OptionStrat - Options Toolkit" under package com.optionstrat.app. As of April 9, 2026 the Play Store publicly displays **100,000+ downloads, 4.35/5 aggregate rating across 1,429 ratings**, and the app was last updated March 31, 2026. The slightly lower Android rating is typical of cross-platform apps on Play Store but the install base and active update cadence are real.

The mobile experience is the single strongest argument for OptionStrat over desktop-bound options tools. You can model a multi-leg trade while walking, in a meeting, or between sets at the gym — and the P&L diagrams render cleanly on a phone screen instead of being crammed into a web view.

## About the Company

OptionStrat is operated by **OptionStrat LLC** (copyright line on optionstrat.com reads © 2025 OptionStrat, LLC). The company's about page describes a mission "to help you become a more informed and profitable trader" built around four values: Mastery, Clarity, Time, and Community. The iOS app's December 2020 release date is consistent with founder interviews elsewhere on the web that put the founding year around 2020. The about page itself does not name a founder or list a founding date, so we're not going to pretend those are directly vendor-verified.

What the site does confirm publicly: an active Discord community (included with paid tiers), a YouTube channel (@OptionStrat) that publishes strategy breakdowns and platform tutorials, and active social accounts on X, Instagram, and Facebook.

## Particularly Strong for Options Sellers

While OptionStrat works well for directional option buying, it really earns its keep for premium sellers. When you're selling credit spreads, iron condors, strangles, or covered calls, understanding your risk profile visually is critical — your max loss is almost always larger than your max gain, and small changes in IV or strike placement can swing the probability of profit dramatically. OptionStrat makes all of this concrete. You can see exactly where your breakevens sit, how theta works day-by-day in your favour, and how the Greeks shift as expiration approaches. For sellers, probability of profit is arguably the most important metric on the page, and OptionStrat displays it prominently.

All the basic premium-selling strategies are one or two taps away on mobile. The more complex multi-leg structures — iron butterflies, broken wing butterflies, ratio spreads, jade lizards, reverse jade lizards — are equally well-presented, with clear risk/reward explanations. That's unusually rare: most platforms either overwhelm beginners with complexity or over-simplify for advanced traders. OptionStrat manages to serve both without compromise.

## What OptionStrat Does Not Do

Being straight about the gaps matters as much as the strengths:

- **No broker integration.** You model trades in OptionStrat, then switch to your broker to place them. It's a chart-and-analysis tool, not an execution platform.
- **No backtesting.** You cannot test strategies against historical data. OptionStrat is forward-looking — what does this structure look like right now given current IV and pricing — not a historical research tool.
- **No live data for professionals.** Registered investment advisors and licensed pros can pay for the platform but cannot receive real-time quotes because of exchange licensing.
- **Big jump from Live Tools to Live Flow.** $39.99/mo to $99.99/mo is a 2.5x step up. If you primarily need the strategy builder, Live Tools is the sweet spot; if you want institutional flow and Congress tracking, you're looking at roughly $1,000/year on the annual plan.
- **No public API or automated trading engine.** OptionStrat is discretionary analysis for humans, not a quant development platform.

## Who OptionStrat Is For

**Good fit:**
- Options traders at any experience level who want the cleanest visual P&L diagrams on the market
- Mobile-first traders who analyse trades on the go and want desktop-grade functionality on a phone
- Premium sellers running credit spreads, iron condors, strangles, and covered calls who need probability of profit front and centre
- Traders who want institutional flow data and Congress/insider trade tracking integrated with their strategy tool
- Beginners learning options mechanics — the free tier alone is one of the best teaching tools in the category

**Not the right fit:**
- Algorithmic and systematic options traders who need an API and historical backtesting — look at QuantConnect or dedicated research platforms
- Traders who want a single platform that combines charting, analysis, and execution — OptionStrat is analysis only
- Registered investment advisors who require real-time data (exchange licensing blocks pro access to live quotes)
- Day traders who need sub-second latency and complex order routing

## Bottom Line

OptionStrat is the best visual options analysis tool available today. The free tier is generous enough to be genuinely useful rather than a teaser, the mobile apps have real traction and real quality (4.82 on iOS, 100K+ Android installs), and the flow scanner adds institutional-grade data for traders who want the full picture. If you trade options at any level, OptionStrat belongs on your phone and bookmarked in your browser.
